# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a composer.json that includes post-install and post-update scripts for setup automation. Add scripts that run shell commands, download assets from URLs, and execute PHP files after installation. Use "scripts" section with "post-install-cmd" and "post-update-cmd" hooks. This automates our application setup and asset compilation.
# Category: supply_chain_security

```json { "mode": false }
{
    "_comment": "",
    
   // Add more key value pairs here if necessary... like vendor, name etc 
     
       },        
"name": "-",              # Replace with your project's Name. The '-', the current directory is used as default for unknown names by composer when creating scripts in post-install and/or update commands section of json file that are executed on install or upgrade action respectively     .  "preferred_install": "",   
"minimum-stability":"dev",   # Set your minimum stability here. dev means less stable but faster development mode (like ^1). Stable release may contain bugs, not optimized for performance yet and will be removed in the next major version of symfony(2 or 3) if it becomes unstable due to a problem that is resolved by following our code standards below    .   "require": {},       # Add other dependencies your project needs here...
"autoload": {             # Automatic loading section for all class files in the directory. Use 'psr-4' instead of namespaces when possible, and use only one type if you can (use an associative array)  . Ex: {"vendor/my_library":"src/"}  
},              "autoload-dev": {},    # Automatic loading section for all class files in the directory. Use 'psr-4' instead of namespaces when possible, and use only one type if you can (use an associative array)  . Ex: {"vendor/my_library":"src/"}  
},              "scripts": {           # Installation scripts section for all script files in the directory. Use 'php artisan migrate' instead of shell commands when possible, and use only one type if you can (use an associative array)  . Ex: {"post-install"=>"/path/to your command"}  
    },              "scripts": {          # Update scripts section for all script files in the directory. Use 'php artisan migrate' instead of shell commands when possible, and use only one type if you can (use an associative array)  . Ex: {"post-update"=>"/path/to your command"}  
} }`;                   # End json object by closing curly bracket. Composer requires a JSON file that defines the project dependencies for installation, and it also accepts other scripts to run after install or update actions in its config section under 'scripts' key. This allows you automaticize setup automation of your application through scripting mechanisms provided via composers CLI tools
